titlestmt.author: Compiled by Julia Parker, Brenda Marston

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-09-06 18:23:39 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Documents, videos, and film from Phil Zwickler's work on "Rights and Reactions," as well as several other projects, including some material regarding "Fear of Disclosure," and miscellaneous files and photographs, mostly from 1987 to 1992.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Mireille Lee, March 2000Peter MartinezSarah Keen, July 2008Sarah Keen, March 2010

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Filmmaker and writer about gay and lesbian issues and the AIDS crisis, born June 1, 1954 and died from the complications of AIDS on May 7, 1991. With Jane Lippman, he produced and directed "Rights and Reactions: Lesbian and Gay Rights on Trial," an award-winning documentary covering the 1986 New York City Council hearings on the gay rights bill. Zwickler collaborated with David Wojnarowicz to produce "Fear of Disclosure," a short film about the issues gay men face dating in the context of AIDS. Zwickler also worked with experimental German film maker, Rosa von Praunheim, to produce "Silence Equals Death" and "Positive," both exploring the emotions and anger of people with AIDS. The film "Positive" includes interviews with Zwickler and two other men who were HIV-positive. He produced his final project, "The Needle Nightmares" (1991), with the help of David Meieran. Zwickler also worked as a news reporter, publishing stories in the Village Voice and serving as editor of the People with AIDS Coalition Newsline for a number of years. Before working in film and journalism, he taught literature in New York City public schools and community colleges.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Documents, publicity, funding proposals, correspondence, production notes, and video outtakes from Phil Zwickler's work on "Rights and Reactions," as well as several other documentary and video projects, including "Fear of Disclosure," "Silence = Death," and "Positive." Also, documentation of his work as editor for "PWA Coalition Newsline," and miscellaneous files and photographs, mostly from 1987 to 1992.

defitem.label: The First 10 Years, GMHC (Gay Men's Health Crisis 1990-1991 Annual Report)
"Image", San Francisco Examiner ("Gay in America", Examiner special report)
Ringling Bros. and Barnum and Bailey Circus (Brochure- Gay Men's Health Crisis Benefit to Fight AIDS)
Good Intentions: How Big Business and the Medical Establishment Are Corrupting the Fight Against AIDS, Alzheimer's, Cancer and More by Bruce Nussbaum
You Can Do Something About AIDS, Sasha Alyson, ed. (publication of the Stop AIDS Project, Inc.)
PWA Coalition Newsline
defitem.item: 1990: Issue 51 (Jan.); Issue 53 (March); Issue 54 (April); Issue 55 (May); Issue 56 (June); Issue 57 (July/Aug.); Issue 58 (Sept.); Issue 59 (Oct.); Issue 60 (Nov.); Issue 61 (Dec.)

defitem.label: PWA Coalition Newsline

defitem.item: 1992: Issue 74 (March); Issue 75 (April); Issue 76 (May); Issue77 (June); Issue 78 (July); Issue 81 (Oct.)1993: Issue 86 (April)

altformavail.head: Available Copies:
altformavail.p: Use copies are available for selected films and videos.
Note:Tapes designated as 3/4"* are 3/4" wide but shorter than regular 3/4" tapes. They can still be viewed using the 3/4" U Matic machine.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Phil Zwickler papers, #7564. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: 29 tape recordings that are part of the "Rights and Reactions" project and may only be screened or viewed for research, scientific, or scholarly purposes. Any and all uses other than the aforementioned are expressly prohibited. No duplication, sale, rental, broadcast, or public screening is authorized

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.

did.unittitle: Resume, Personal narrative, and proposals for "Fear of disclosure"
Memorial service items and obituaries - collected by archivistPhotographs, contact sheets, negatives

Herstory (tape 33): #7 - City Council hearing on Gay Rights Bill 2/22/83. Includes: Concluding speeches of Friedlander + Messinger. Roll call. Speech by Long. Audience chants "Justice!" w/ banner singing, pink papers triangles on floor.

Herstory (tape 34): #8 City Council hearing on Gay Rights Bill 2/22/83; Violence Against Lesbians tape: Outside City Hall: Police put arrested people into van. Banner inside van. Interview with Pat B.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A portion of this interview is available at www.pzfoundation.org

did.unittitle: Speeches and newspaper headlines that occurred during the time of the passage of the Gay Rights bill
